Policy is sound and conservative — no change warranted. The DOWN SPY regime further increases overnight gap-down risk.

notes_for_next_session:
FOR PREMARKET AGENT (8:30 AM ET Monday 2026-06-29). Evening scan complete.

BOOK STATE: Flat. 19 consecutive no-entry sessions. Equity $99,938.80.

WATCHLIST: 6 candidates (PANW, MGM, EIX, ES, D, AMGN). Thin list — market not in broad RS-leader expansion. Three utilities (EIX, ES, D) reflect defensive rotation, not growth leadership. Apply extra scrutiny to these.

PRIORITY ORDER FOR MONDAY:
1. PANW (score 17.47) — strongest tech setup. RS=1.117, VCP=0.795, 0.7% from 52w high, vol_dry=0.710. Close $303.60 cleared prev_high $295.53 Friday. BUT: has gap-faded every day this week. RSI gate and ATR%≥0.30% check are mandatory before any entry. Skip if RSI >60 at open.
2. MGM (score 17.08) — best RS at 1.167, best vol dry-up at 0.659. 4.7% from 52w high. Has triggered gap-up trap 3+ times recently. If weekend consolidation reset the extension and RSI is 55-60, this is highest conviction. Do not chase if RSI >60.
3. EIX (score 15.32) — borderline setup: VCP=0.813 close to 0.85 ceiling, vol_dry=0.895 close to 0.90 ceiling. Utility. Lower confidence; only enter if PANW and MGM fail gates and EIX checks all criteria cleanly.
4. ES (score 12.99) — Eversource (utility), RS improved to 1.115 vs 1.074 last week. Past ATR% failures remain a concern. Third priority only.
5. D (FLAGGED, score 12.08) — Dominion Energy. RS=1.075 barely above 1.05 minimum, well below 1.10 ideal. VCP=0.847 borderline. Down day Friday. Lowest confidence; do not prioritize.
6. AMGN (score 6.78) — 8.5% from 52w high, weakest VCP. Score gap vs others is large. Skip unless all higher-ranked names fail gates.

NON-NEGOTIABLE GATES (no exceptions):
- RSI 55-60 at entry (ONLY profitable bucket; RSI >60 = hard skip)
- ATR% ≥ 0.30% at time of entry (every trade below floor has failed)
- Price above prev_day_high for breakout confirmation
